summary.intent: Investigate and fix failing CI checks (Adapter Parity and Adapter Tests) on PR #732 after a prior review-fix push, then get Codex to approve the PR.
summary.outcome: Adapter Parity CI failure was fixed by adding a missing Leptos wasm test counterpart for a Dioxus-only smoke path, bringing the tabs parity delta from 3 to 2 (within the 2-tolerance threshold). The commit was staged and ready to push.

repo: The adapter parity gate counts public symbols and test attributes per adapter (Leptos vs Dioxus) per component and fails if the delta exceeds a configured tolerance (2 for tabs).
CI has distinct jobs: 'Adapter Parity' (symbol/test count symmetry) and 'Adapter Tests' (actual test execution); both were failing but only Parity was a real code issue.
Dioxus wasm tests for Tabs had more coverage than Leptos counterparts, specifically browser/wasm smoke tests for core-attribute paths, causing parity to drift.
The xtask CI commands are: `cargo xtask ci adapter-parity` for the count gate and `cargo xtask ci adapter` for the test runner.
Codex review is triggered via `@codex review` PR comment; active review is signaled by `chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]:eyes` reaction, approval by `:+1`.
Review threads must be explicitly resolved via GitHub GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation after replying; resolution is not automatic.

code.finding: New wasm test `wasm_attrs_observable_through_machine_connect_api` added to mirror the Dioxus counterpart, fixing the parity delta from 3 to 2.
Adapter parity logic lives here; counts test attributes across all adapter test files matching a component name.

workflow: GitHub Actions job logs can return 404 blobs for in-progress or recently-failed jobs; using `gh run view --job --log` with grep filtering is more reliable than direct API log blob fetches.
Local reproduction of CI gates (`cargo xtask ci adapter-parity`) is faster and more reliable than reading truncated GitHub Actions log output.
The Adapter Tests job appeared failed in CI but passed locally — likely a hosted-runner transient or log artifact, not a real code failure.
When a wasm test sandbox blocks `wasm-bindgen-test-runner` from spawning its browser server, running with elevated permissions resolves it.

friction: GitHub Actions job log blob returned 404 for the Adapter Tests job, requiring fallback to `gh run view --job --log` which produced extremely verbose output requiring grep filtering.
The Adapter Tests CI failure could not be definitively confirmed as a transient vs real failure because the log was inaccessible; had to rely on local reproduction passing as a proxy.
The wasm test runner was blocked by sandbox permissions on the first attempt, requiring a second run with escalation.
Polling Codex review status required manually sleeping 300s in a terminal loop; there is no push-based notification mechanism for Codex reactions.
Codex did not immediately start reviewing after the first `@codex review` trigger; a second nudge was required ~10 minutes later.
open_items: The CI fix commit was staged but not yet pushed at the end of the transcript — PR #732 still needs the push to trigger a new CI run.
The Adapter Tests CI failure root cause was not definitively identified (transient runner issue vs real failure); worth monitoring the next CI run to confirm it self-resolves.
Codex had approved PR #732 before the CI investigation began, but the new commit will require another Codex review pass.
